extra.reasoning: This is a state machine. The property G(opted_out -> not sent) requires sent to be false in every reachable state where opted_out is true. The transition t_resume_glitch directly breaks this: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and ticks >= 3), it sets sent := true while opted_out remains true. Reaching its guard is easy: optout sets opted_out=true (sent=false, ticks=0); three ticks raise ticks to 1, 2, 3 (each tick also keeps sent=false and leaves opted_out=true). Now opted_out=true and ticks=3. On send_attempt, t_resume_glitch is enabled (opted_out true and 3>=3) and its update sets sent:=true, yielding the state opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=3 — which violates opted_out -> not sent. Although t_send_blocked is also enabled on this event (and would keep sent=false), the property quantifies over every reachable execution; the nondeterministic choice of t_resume_glitch is a valid enabled transition producing a reachable violating state. The counterexample uses 5 events, within the 7-event bound.
